mnemonics: STACKS & QUEUES: FIFO/LIFO - First In, First Out (Queue like a line of people), Last In, First Out (Stack like a stack of plates)
keyPoints: Understand the core operations (push, pop, enqueue, dequeue) and their time complexities
Know the differences between arrays and linked lists as underlying implementations
Be able to trace through algorithm execution on these structures (e.g., for a series of push/pop operations)
commonMistakes: Confusing the order of operations for a stack (LIFO) vs. a queue (FIFO)
Forgetting to check for an empty stack/queue before a pop/dequeue operation, leading to underflow errors
Incorrectly implementing pointers/references in linked list-based queues, especially for the rear node
